cards.definition: the systems model for electronic and mechanical products — input triggers a process that produces an output
an input device that detects a change in the environment — e.g. LDR (light), thermistor (temperature), microphone (sound)
a component whose resistance decreases as light intensity increases — used in light-sensitive circuits
a component whose resistance changes with temperature — used in temperature-sensing circuits
an electronic component used as a switch or amplifier — turns a circuit on when the base current reaches a threshold
a programmable chip that can control electronic systems — e.g. Arduino. Takes inputs and controls outputs based on programmed logic
Computer-Aided Design software is used to create designs, which are then manufactured using Computer-Aided Manufacture machines like laser cutters and CNC routers
a CAM machine that cuts or engraves materials using a focused laser beam — precise and quick for sheet materials
a CAM machine that builds objects layer by layer from a digital design — used for prototyping and small batch production
a process where a heated thermoplastic sheet is stretched over a mould and a vacuum pulls it tight — used for packaging and trays
